title: Docspell
description: Self-hosted document organizer (DMS) that auto-tags, OCRs and full-text-searches your scanned PDFs, emails and uploads.
installation: This is the Docspell rest server (web UI + REST API) listening on internal port 7880. It REQUIRES a companion job executor deployed alongside it for OCR, text extraction and document conversion: deploy docspell/joex:latest pointed at the SAME Postgres add-on (set its DOCSPELL_JOEX_JDBC_* / DOCSPELL_JOEX_BASE__URL env). Both containers must share the file storage; this template stores binaries in the bundled Postgres (DOCSPELL_SERVER_BACKEND_FILES_DEFAULT__STORE=database), so no shared volume between server and joex is needed for files. Rotate the random secrets before production: DOCSPELL_SERVER_AUTH_SERVER__SECRET (openssl rand -hex 32) and DOCSPELL_SERVER_ADMIN__ENDPOINT_SECRET, and rotate the Postgres password. Full-text search defaults here to the built-in PostgreSQL FTS backend (DOCSPELL_SERVER_FULL__TEXT__SEARCH_ENABLED=true + DOCSPELL_SERVER_FULL__TEXT__SEARCH_BACKEND=postgresql), so no external Apache Solr is needed; only add a Solr instance if you set the backend to solr. First run: open the web UI, register a new account using the same name for both 'collective' and 'user', then log in. Optional companions for inbound mail/scanner ingest (docspell-consumedir, IMAP poller) are described in the docs and reuse the same Postgres.
